dialogue.utterance: Truck 3, respond to 1517 [STREET_ADDRESS] for a report of a stroke in an 88 year old female.
Hi, my name is Alex from Rescue Squad. I'm here to help-can I check on your mother first?
Her name is Sarah. She was using the bathroom, then slumped over and became less alert. We are worried about a stroke.
Mrs. Sarah, can you tell me your name? Partner: No response at all, just groaned when prompted.
Partner, was she conscious enough to open her eyes? Partner: Yes, she opened her eyes when spoken to.
Did she make any verbal responses when you spoke to her? Partner: Only nonverbal responses-groans.
Did she move when you offered reassurance earlier? Partner: Yes, she moved both arms slightly but had trouble with the lower legs at first.
Let's check her breathing and prepare to take blood pressure.
Respirations are 22, depth normal, and unlabored.
Pulse is steady, 58, and strong. BP is 159 over 86.
Has she reported any pain recently? Family Member: She has not indicated any pain since the event began.
What's her medical history or regular medications? Family Member: She has dementia and uses medications like amiodarone and [CITY].
Anything about allergies or other medications? Partner: No allergies reported. No verbal confirmation from the patient.
Assess her pupils. Pupil size and reactivity. Partner: Pupils are 3mm, equal, reactive to light.

dialogue.role: nose, mouth and throat exam; Partner
dialogue.utterance: No facial drooping noted. She's tracking with her eyes, but not speaking.
Breathing is unlabored. What about oxygen and EKG? Partner: SpO2 is 96% on room air. EKG is in sinus rhythm.
Are her arms symmetrical? Partner: No arm drift noted, but grip strength is weak and equal on both.
She shows trouble with speech, but eye tracking is intact.
Her GCS is 12-baseline is 14 due to dementia.
Pulse is 58 with regular rhythm.
Respiratory rate is 22, regular.
Blood pressure is 159/86.
Oxygen saturation is 96%.
EKG shows sinus rhythm, borderline bradycardia noted.
Tympanic temperature is 97.4°F.
Blood glucose is 92-within normal limits.
IV access placed in left antecubital vein-preferred extremity vein for entry due to ease and compliance of patient skin.
4-lead EKG placed as per protocol for stroke assessment and monitoring.
Protocol 3-5 applied for possible stroke-due to cognitive change, aphasic presentation, and age-related factors for ischemic stroke risk.
Pulse still 58, RR 22, BP unchanged, and SpO2 still 96%. Stable for immediate transport to CT facility [COMPANY_NAME].
Transporting to [COMPANY_NAME] for a CT scan due to stroke concerns-selected as closest stroke center per local protocol.
Secure her to the stretcher to prepare for transport-used in-line stabilization given baseline weakness and altered mental status.
She is not alert and oriented with GCS of 12-presentation includes progression from baseline GCS of 14, suggesting acute deficit consistent with stroke.
No verbal response to commands-consistent with aphasia, given her non-verbal response, but no known baseline prior to current epistaxis.
At facility, she appeared more alert-suggesting possible gradual neurological posturing or varying baseline over transport time.
Stable during transport-no major changes observed en route-documented per stroke transport time guidelines for consideration of thrombolytics if stroke is confirmed.
[UNIQUE_IDENTIFIER] not obtained in documentation-patient is under "unable to obtain" due to current AMS and family unable to provide due to own time constraints during event call.
No NP name provided; facility signature missing-standard nursing templates: "unable to sign" due to time pressure post-hospital entry.
Family member Mr. B listed as contact for follow-up-no proxies confirmed from prior history given age and uncooperative baseline.
Baseline GCS of 14-documented for reference in case note to denote decline of motor and verbal from 6 and 2, respectively, from full 15 per family input.

Unable to obtain further neurological input from the patient-per guidelines, GCS and physical replications were the priority due to baseline educational limitations of family to assist beyond visual tracking and grip involvement.
Patient unable to respond to commands in documentation-no reliable mental component for MV testing aside from approximation to Herman's grading standard .
IV start uneventful; flush and restart done successfully-kept in left AC due to right arm rigidity and home compliance with known left-side flexibility from past home visits reported by family .

EMS documentation vs. hospital intake report to be cross-verified for missing fields-standard procedure for stroke teams to coordinate failure prioritization with transfer physicians in case of altered outcomes .
Document states "Unable to obtain patient information due to altered mental status"-proper documentation per patient constraints .
EKG copied to hospital-no acute changes noted-listed as sinus rhythm borderline bradycardia, per field consideration .
Medications include tramadol, Tylenol, Cordarone, [CITY], Vesicare, and Pravachol-as verified by family of stock medications .
Baseline GCS of 14 is noted per family's history-per their recall of functional scoring during awake portions of day .
Inform receiving hospital about missing [UNIQUE_IDENTIFIER] and signatures-per protocol "unable to obtain" compliant for follow-ups .
All protocols for stroke followed-transport under time guidelines-expectation is within golden hour standards .
Continue communication with hub for intake confirmation-used to ensure expeditious interventional scheduling .
Signs of localized weakness and speech issues-consistent with stroke presentation assessed from left arm weakness and personnel verbal failure .
Recommend hospital staff complete neurological evaluation per protocol-especially FAST or NIHSS per their standard intake .
Equal pulses noted-no edema or swelling observed-due to ongoing acute stroke presentation that has not caused compartment signs .
No noted cramps or joint issues-validating base mobility .
Blood glucose in normal range-no diabetes signs-must be verified to rule out hypoglycemic event .
No signs of trauma or sudden injury .
Stroke parameters met for possible thrombolytic treatment-inform receiving staff .
Cotter pin brought to hospital in case of need for line access-documented as stock per transport field norm .
Unlikely to be definitive aphasia but communication is limited-could be global aphasia assuming left hemisphere events .
Patient groans in response but doesn't form words-consistent with aphasia .
Both arms showed some grip strength-left arm not stronger than right .
Pupils equal and reactivity normal but moderate limb weakness-no rubor or cyanosis noted .
Change from baseline GCS 14 to current 12-documented .
Initial resistance in lower leg movement, but showed progress .
No acute worsening of lower ext-no dragging noted .
Following stroke protocols-documented each step in field notes-specific to 4 lead monitoring and stroke signage .
Transport duration 30 minutes-falls under stroke alert protocol .
No known dysphagia or expression issues-baseline stable .
GCS indicating mild mental change-limits full neurological testing .
Groans not meaningful speech-suggests left hemisphere involvement .
No face-to-face confirmation of baseline or medications-document limitations .
Exclude information not in standardized fields until hospital provides intake .
No swelling noted, but posture indicates some pre-existing postural issues .
IV fluid administered without verbal feedback-attentive to any resistance .
Restraint used en route due to AMS and non-verbal behavior .
Chest without JVD or structural issues-no palpable deformities found .
Normal breath sounds-no wheeze or indications of atelectasis .
Eyes track well but no vocal speech-only bilateral groans .
No delays identified in emergency handling or assessment .
Stroke risk factors like age and anticoagulants relevant .
Cognitive baseline of 14-change to 12 is a concern .
Emphasize hospital to monitor for early stroke progression .
No baseline data before acute event known-family not certain .
Previous vitals undocumented-assume family not with patient at onset .
Pulse remains stable in normal sinus throughout transport .
No signs of hypotension or tachycardia-vitals stable .
Glucose maintained normal-consistent with baseline range .
Early intervention aligns with acute stroke guidelines .
No facial droop identified-verbal expression is absent .
No known head injury documented-age elevates stroke probability .
Recommend hospital performs FAST exam for confirmation .
EMS did not assess for sensory deficits-unconfirmed .
All interventions during transport safe and targeted-per standard stroke alerts .
No need for boluses under current stability .
Reassessment every 5 minutes per field procedure-per stroke event documentation .
No IV meds given besides maintenance saline .
Approximate event onset-30 mins ago .
No prior change in consciousness or fainting-per family only .
No red flags for early contra-indications to stroke therapy .
